dyna1_feature: Dyna-1 flags pocket-lining residues in µs–ms exchange; pocket opens only in a minor state
evidence: KRAS switch-II pocket (undruggable→sotorasib); PocketMiner (Meller & Bowman 2023) predicts cryptic sites from dynamics
mechanism: A pocket absent in the crystal but sampled on µs–ms is invisible to static docking — the #1 reason a target is called 'undruggable'

dyna1_feature: Residues undergoing the active↔inactive transition light up; drug selects the minor state
evidence: Imatinib selects inactive DFG-out Abl (Kern 2014); type-II kinase inhibitors generally
mechanism: Selectivity between structurally identical pockets comes from which CONFORMATION each protein can access, not the static pocket

dyna1_feature: Allosteric pockets are defined by coupled µs–ms motion, not a static cleft
evidence: PTP1B allosteric site; SHP2 (allosteric drugs); Dyna-1 flags the coupled network residues
mechanism: Allosteric sites are dynamics constructs — you cannot see them without the motion that couples them to the active site

dyna1_feature: Affinity set by population of the bindable state × intrinsic pocket fit; Dyna-1 gives the state axis
evidence: Abl vs Src: identical crystal pocket, 3000× affinity gap from µs–ms dynamics (Kern 2014); Boltz-2 can't split them (9.5 vs 8.2)
mechanism: Thermodynamic affinity depends on accessible-state populations — the dynamics sets ΔG that a single structure cannot

dyna1_feature: Slow conformational trapping (induced-fit closure) lengthens koff; koff is itself a µs–ms rate
evidence: Copeland drug–target residence-time theory; long koff tracks in-vivo efficacy better than Kd
mechanism: Residence time IS a kinetic/dynamic observable; the property in vivo efficacy correlates with is the one dynamics governs, not Kd

dyna1_feature: Dynamics adds the conformational-state axis on top of sequence/pocket differences
evidence: Kinome selectivity: state access separates near-identical ATP pockets
mechanism: Sequence and pocket shape do most of the work; dynamics resolves the cases where those are degenerate

dyna1_feature: Off-targets often share an accessible conformational state, not just a sequence motif
evidence: Kinase polypharmacology tracks shared DFG-out accessibility
mechanism: Profiling which off-targets can reach the same state narrows liability beyond a static similarity search

dyna1_feature: CDR-H3 and framework loops undergo µs–ms exchange; Dyna-1 flags functional loops it was trained on
evidence: CDR-H3 conformational heterogeneity ↔ polyreactivity/aggregation; paratope flexibility ↔ affinity maturation
mechanism: Antibody 'motion' is real and measurable, but developability also has orthogonal drivers (charge patch, hydrophobicity) dynamics won't see

dyna1_feature: Gatekeeper/allosteric mutations act by SHIFTING the conformational equilibrium a drug relies on
evidence: Abl T315I; kinase gatekeeper mutations re-populate the drug-disfavored state
mechanism: A mutation that changes no contact can still kill a drug by moving the population — a dynamics effect

dyna1_feature: Only via target engagement + residence time; most of efficacy is pathway biology
evidence: Residence-time→efficacy link (above); everything else is network/PK context
mechanism: Efficacy is an organismal outcome; dynamics touches one input (engagement kinetics), not the whole chain

dyna1_feature: Dynamics informs ONLY the off-target sliver of tox; endpoints are chemistry + physiology
evidence: hERG = channel pharmacology; DILI = reactive metabolites/immune; no µs–ms backbone signal
mechanism: Claiming dynamics predicts toxicity is overreach — it predicts one upstream contributor (off-target binding), not the endpoint

dyna1_feature: Cost is synthesis route, CMC, formulation, trial size — no protein-dynamics content
evidence: Small-molecule COGS and biologic manufacturing are process-chemistry problems
mechanism: Difficulty-to-DRUG (dynamics-informed) is NOT difficulty-to-MAKE (chemistry/process) — conflating them is a category error

property: Chemical potency vs a single static structure
dyna1_feature: Docking one crystal and ranking by score is exactly what misses the dynamics
evidence: FeatureDock/Boltz-2 rank the wrong state; Abl≈Src on static score despite 3000× gap
mechanism: The property people compute (static-structure IC50) is the one dynamics most contradicts — this is the whole thesis

dyna1_feature: Governed by ligand physchem (logP, PSA, solubility), not target backbone motion
mechanism: Nothing in the target's µs–ms exchange sets the drug's absorption
